passage.title: Trip Map Trial
passage.text: Our field trip team tested two ways to walk through the museum last month. Lina used a full map with every room, but Noah used a simple route strip. After one long morning, Noah's group got lost less often because the route strip was easier to follow. The teachers timed each route and marked the stops on a chart. When Lina saw the difference, she changed to a simple route strip. During the next walk, her team missed fewer turns. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that one clear route at a time can make travel easier.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Testing an easier route system for a field trip
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: What did Noah's group use to walk through the museum?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does route mean in the first sentence?
choices: A person who visits a club
The way from one place to another
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Lina switch to a simple route strip?
choices: The first tool was missing from the class
She thought the shorter guide would reduce confusion
The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
